---
title: "03_Analyses"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r Load}

library(tidyverse)
library(vegan) # for NMDS

# polen data
pollen_samples <- read_csv('Data/Pollen_and_Surveys/Pollen_samples_data_cleaned.csv')
# nest-level data summary
beehav_sum <- read_csv('Data/Behaviour/Behaviour_nest_summary_cleaned.csv')
# nest-level data all social statuses
beehav_full <- read_csv('Data/Behaviour/Behaviour_nest_full_cleaned.csv')
# pollen_samples and behaviour data
pollen_beehav <- read_csv('Data/Behaviour/Pollen_and_behaviour_cleaned.csv')


```

```{r Sociality}

table(beehav_sum$Num_females_same_day)
# ignore: 10 nests had 0 observed females partaking in nest care
# 36 had 1 female at a given time partaking in nest care
# 3 nests had 2 females
# 2 nests had 3 females
# 2+3 / 36+2+3 = 12% are social

# mean number of females
beehav_sum %>% filter(Social_status_con == 'social')
# (2 + 2 + 2 + 3 + 3)/ 5  = 2.4

# first social nest
beehav_full %>% filter(Social_status_con == 'social')
# 2024-11-14 

# were these nests just social, or both social and solitary?
social_nests <- beehav_full %>% filter(Nest == 'J100' | Nest == 'J18' | Nest == 'J87' | Nest == 'J91' | Nest == 'J92')

# J100: 
 # 2025-01-16 - solitary for 1 day
 # 2025-02-04 - social for 1 day

# J18: 
 # 2024-11-14 to 2024-11-25 - social for 12 days (solitary for one day in between)
 # 2024-12-02 to 2025-01-16 - solitary for 45 days

# J87
 # 2024-12-17 to 2025-01-06 - solitary for 20 days
 # 2025-01-07 - social for 1 day
 # 2025-01-15 to 2025-02-04 - solitary for 20 days

# J91
 # 2025-01-16 to 2025-01-22 - social for 7 days
 # not active in subsequent recordings

# J92
 # 2025-01-16 to 2025-01-28 - social for 13 days


```

```{r Pollen_Collection}

## Flower Constancy

# how many samples are above 95% of one pollen type
pollen_samples %>% filter(Count_scaled >= 285)
# 46 out of 83 samples 


## How many samples and individuals 

# how many
length(unique(pollen_samples$Bee_ID))
# 49 individuals, 83 samples, ranges 1-4 samples per bee


## NMDS for pollen composition variation among individuals 

# pivot wider
pollen_samples_wide <- pollen_samples %>% select(Bee_ID, Slide_code, Subtype, Count_scaled)
pollen_samples_wide <- pivot_wider(pollen_samples_wide, names_from = 'Subtype', values_from = 'Count_scaled', id_cols = c(Bee_ID, Slide_code))

# replace NA with 0
pollen_samples_wide[is.na(pollen_samples_wide)] <- 0

# # remove unknown types --- not for the composition question! but yes for abundannce question 
# pollen_samples_wide <- pollen_samples_wide %>% select(-`Unknown 1`, -`Unknown 2`, -`Unknown 3`, -`Unknown 4`, -`Unknown 5`, -`Unknown 6`, -`Unknown 7`)
# # now some samples have a sum of 0 (if they had only unknown pollen types) so remove them for numeric columns
# pollen_samples_wide <- pollen_samples_wide[rowSums(pollen_samples_wide[, sapply(pollen_samples_wide, is.numeric)], na.rm = TRUE) > 0, ]

# extract composition data for one df then descriptors for the other
# taxa <- pollen_samples_wide %>% select(Ranunculus:Rhododendron)
desc <- pollen_samples_wide %>% select(Bee_ID, Slide_code)

## create NMDS
# nmds_pollen_comp <- metaMDS(taxa, distance = 'bray', k = 2)


nmds_pollen_comp <- metaMDS(pollen_samples_wide[,3:30], distance = 'bray', k = 2)


## Inspect
print(nmds_pollen_comp) # stress is tiny....
stressplot(nmds_pollen_comp) # should look like stairs

plot(nmds_pollen_comp)
scores(nmds_pollen_comp)


# Extract NMDS scores for bees 
  nmds_sample_scores <-
    # get nmds scores 
    as.data.frame(scores(nmds_pollen_comp)$sites) 
  
  # missing columns I want
nmds_sample_scores$Slide_code <- pollen_samples_wide$Slide_code
  
  
  
nmds_sample_scores <- left_join(nmds_sample_scores, desc)
  
  
nmds_sample_scores %>% 
  group_by(Bee_ID) %>% 
  filter(n() >= 2) %>% # try bees with more than one sample
  ungroup() %>% 
ggplot() + 
  # add site scores
  geom_point(aes(x=NMDS1, y=NMDS2, colour = Bee_ID), 
             size = 2) + 
  geom_text(aes(x=NMDS1, y=NMDS2, label = Bee_ID)) + # add species scores
  theme_classic()
#### the groupped together chunk is flower constant samples, even of different compositions 






# try NDMS for floral abundance 





```

```{r}


```
